steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage establishes that former Spanish colonies still speak Spanish (Cuba example), then shifts to the Netherlands, which also left the Spanish Empire but where Spanish is rarely spoken. The blank must signal this opposite outcome.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — This contrast is evident in the Netherlands,: This simply restates that the Netherlands was a colony without signaling the contrast in language outcome. ✗
  • Choice B — Another example is the Netherlands,: "Another example" incorrectly presents the Netherlands as another case like Cuba, but the sentence says Spanish is rarely spoken there. ✗
  • Choice C — The reverse is true of the Netherlands,: Explicitly signals a flip from the Cuba example to its opposite outcome. ✓
  • Choice D — The results can be heard in the Netherlands,: "The results can be heard" mentions language indirectly but does not clearly signal the opposite language outcome. ✗
That matches Choice C — The reverse is true of the Netherlands,.
